title: Fix Monster Island provenance disposition review findings
description:
Adversarial review found Monster Island bounded reference evidence was marked governed-now while its authority_state is reference_authoritative_not_app_promoted. Fix the semantic contract so reference-only evidence remains app-promotion blocked, add validator/test coverage for governed-now requiring app-facing authority, ensure evidence_paths are committed/tracked, and rerun source/provenance/page/vision validators plus node test-chargen.js. Context: parent r4bx Monster Island source-attestation work; do not promote STARTING_SPIRITS or app-facing data in this bug fix.

## Required Skills
ce-simplify-code,compound-engineering:ce-correctness-reviewer,compound-engineering:ce-adversarial-reviewer
acceptance_criteria: Actual Monster Island legacy disposition no longer claims governed-now app authority while app promotion remains blocked; validate_provenance rejects governed-now payloads with reference-only/not-app-promoted authority_state; tests verify evidence artifacts exist and are git-tracked with matching source_revision_id; all existing validators and node test-chargen.js pass.
notes:
Final adversarial review found additional evidence contract gaps: validate_provenance must reject untracked evidence_paths, stale evidence artifact source_revision_id, mismatched artifact source_id, and unconventional non-app-facing authority_state aliases such as reference_only/internal_reference_only. Fixing in-flight under this bug bead before closure.
Final allowlist adversarial review found two additional P0 escapes: non-boolean source_authority values bypassed governed authority validation, and app-facing authority_state values were accepted under non-governed dispositions. Fixed by enforcing source_authority boolean type and requiring app-facing allowlisted authority states to appear only with governed-now dispositions, with regression tests.
Final3 adversarial review found all-caps/broken-case authority_state bypasses. Fixed by rejecting source_authority true claims whose authority_state is neither a recognized reference state nor an explicit governed app-facing allowlist state, with regressions for ACCEPTEDFORAPP/REFERENCEONLY/APPFACING/APPPROMOTED/GOVERNEDAPPAUTHORITY and broken mixed-case variants.
Final4 adversarial review found simultaneous disposition+authority_state promotion could skip evidence validation. Fixed by requiring governed source_authority claims with source_ids to validate evidence_paths and reject reference-only evidence_state for app-facing source authority, with regression for promoting Monster Island to accepted_for_app while its evidence_state remains reference_only.
Final5 adversarial review found governed source authority with empty/null source_ids could skip app-facing evidence_state validation. Fixed by requiring governed source_authority claims to validate evidence state regardless of source_ids presence, with regression for governed accepted_for_app Monster Island payload without explicit source_ids.
close_reason: Completed: fixed provenance disposition and authority validation findings; Monster Island remains reference-only/app-promotion-blocked, evidence artifacts are tracked and validated, and source/provenance/page/vision/ingest/test checks pass.

title: Investigate whether Animism, Sorcery, and Mysticism should be cult-gated
description: Research whether Animism, Sorcery, and Mysticism are inherently cult-based magic schools or independent magic systems that may be taught by cults/organizations in some sources and independently in others. Compare Mythras Core rules, Monster Island, Adventures in Glorantha/God Forgot, Arkat/Zzistori/Waha context, and existing app/source records. Determine whether Step 9 should spin Animism, Sorcery, and Mysticism out from cult selection gating, and produce implementation recommendations plus required source/provenance/test changes. Do not promote source facts without the repo source-attestation chain; cite exact source IDs/page/block evidence where available. This investigation should come after mythras-chargen-s7c1 and mythras-chargen-3zx3, and sit around/coterminous with mythras-chargen-2yg7.5.
acceptance_criteria: Investigation compares Mythras Core Animism, Sorcery, and Mysticism source records; Monster Island animism/spirit-cult pages; AiG/God Forgot/Zzistori/Arkat/Waha app-source records; and current Step 9 implementation. It must distinguish RAW magic-system access from cult/organization teaching/access, cite exact source ids/page/block evidence where available, identify source gaps/blocked evidence, decide whether Animism/Sorcery/Mysticism should be represented independently from cult selection, and specify required reference JSON, provenance, app UI/API, test, and migration changes before implementation.
notes:
2026-05-22 Copilot: created from user request to investigate whether Animism, Sorcery, and Mysticism are independent Mythras magic systems rather than cult-gated choices. Dependencies intentionally make this follow AiG source authority (s7c1) and Waha reconciliation (3zx3); it is placed under parent 2yg7 and blocks/coordinates with Mysticism implementation 2yg7.5 so Step 9 access-model work is informed by source comparison before app behavior changes.
2026-05-22 status correction: blocked until prerequisite source-authority Beads s7c1 (AiG) and 3zx3 (Waha) are unblocked/accepted. The investigation should compare Mythras Core plus Monster Island, AiG/God Forgot, Arkat/Zzistori/Waha, and current app behavior before changing magic access. It remains intentionally positioned before/blocking 2yg7.5 so Mysticism implementation is informed by source comparison.
2026-05-22 dependency-cycle fix: removed mistaken dependency on mythras-chargen-2yg7.5. The intended ordering is w6si investigates Animism/Sorcery/Mysticism access model after source prerequisites, and 2yg7.5 remains blocked by w6si before Mysticism app behavior is implemented.
2026-05-22 dependency hygiene: broad AiG U5 mythras-chargen-s7c1 remains open/blocked for all-page AiG coverage, but w6si's bounded AiG magic-access prerequisite is satisfied by closed beads mythras-chargen-s7c1.4 (extraction) and mythras-chargen-14mn (independent verification) covering pages 31, 32, 60, 61, 62. Removed direct dependency on broad s7c1 so the access-model investigation can proceed with the verified bounded pages while not implying all-page AiG completion.
2026-05-22 investigation conclusion: Animism, Sorcery, and Mysticism are not inherently cult-based magic schools. Mythras Core presents them as independent magic systems whose starting access depends on culture, career, and setting accessibility, while cult membership is optional and provides training/magic/social networks (references/mythras-raw/magic-overview.json, p.34-37). Core Animism has Trance/Binding mechanics (references/mythras-raw/animism.json, p.131-154). Core Sorcery has Invocation/Shaping, source types, and schools (references/mythras-raw/sorcery.json, p.162-178). Core Mysticism remains a target system but references/mythras-raw/mysticism.json is currently verified=false, so it cannot be promoted yet.

Source comparison: cults/traditions/schools are access providers, not the systems themselves. Monster Island bounded evidence supports animism via totemic/spirit cult structures and ranks (references/sources/pages/monster-island.json pdf pages 133-140, 285-286), but remains non-app-facing while permission/lifecycle promotion is pending. Waha is a verified hybrid theist/animist cult-backed example with Devotion, Runic Affinity, and Trance (references/cults-raw/praxian/waha.json; references/sources/pages/waha.json). Daka Fal is an ancestor/spirit-society example with Binding/Trance in legacy one-pager data (references/cults-raw/praxian/daka-fal.json). Arkat is a sorcery cult example with Invocation/Shaping (references/cults-raw/darkness/arkat.json). God Forgot/Zzistori is the key non-cult example: ADR-0010 and current app code already model it as culture/career/no-cult backed sorcery through CULTURE_MAGIC_PROFILES plus resolveCultureBackedSorcerySource()/resolveActiveSorcerySource(), not as a cult.

AiG bounded evidence: pages 31, 60, 61, and 62 are verified contributing magic-access pages under source revision aig:copyparty-sources-books-adventures-in-glorantha-gencon-preview-pdf:0edc1e549c56:2026-05-22 with extraction and independent verification artifacts at references/sources/evidence/aig/page-0031-*, page-0060-*, page-0061-*, and page-0062-*. Page 32 is verified non-contributing. These pages support ADR-level reasoning, but their derived_facts remain empty and app-facing promotion remains blocked by ADR-0012/source-chain gates.

Implementation recommendation: do not add a generic free-standing Animism/Sorcery/Mysticism picker. Do not keep the systems conceptually cult-gated either. Model systems independently, and expose access only through source-backed providers: cults, spirit traditions/societies, sorcery schools, mystic orders, species/culture/career rules, or other cited paths. Keep detectCultType() as the cult-backed provider classifier. Keep ADR-0010's God Forgot/Zzistori no-cult school-backed sorcery path. Future Step 9/provider work must be fail-closed: no provider, no picker; provider spell/spirit/talent lists stay provider-scoped; no unrestricted Core fallback unless the provider source explicitly grants it.

Follow-up created: mythras-chargen-2yg7.8 verifies Mythras Core Mysticism pages and blocks mythras-chargen-2yg7.5. ADR-0013 records the durable access-model decision and amends ADR-0006's stale Mysticism promotion status; ADR-0006 now links that amendment.
close_reason: Investigation completed; decision captured in ADR-0013, Mysticism verification blocker mythras-chargen-2yg7.8 created, and implementation recommendation recorded.

title: fix: Correct ~400 garbled OCR entries across all cult MIRACLES_DATA, reference JSONs, and inline constants
notes:
## OCR Garbage Cleanup Plan

### Scope
~400 entries flagged with `split_from_garbled: true` and/or `rune_inferred: true` across
the MIRACLES_DATA inline constant in index.html. These are OCR artifacts from cult one-pager
PDF extractions.

### Examples Found
- "gow Fear 4 Summon Lune" → should be "Fear" + "Summon Lune" (fixed for Jakaleel)
- "o Summon Shade" → should be "Summon Shade"
- "Behold" → not a real miracle (OCR noise)
- Prefixes like "gow", "o", "R", "RS", "jo", "j", "i" are OCR-garbled rune abbreviations

### Approach
1. For each cult with garbled entries, compare inline data against:
   - The per-cult reference JSON (references/cults-raw/<pantheon>/<cult>.json)
   - The source PDF if available (references/cults-upstream/)
2. Extract correct miracle names from reference JSON cultMiracles field
3. Replace garbled inline entries with verified data
4. Remove split_from_garbled/rune_inferred flags from corrected entries
5. Vision-mode verify against source PDF pages where uncertain

### ADR-003 Compliance
All corrections must trace to the reference JSON. If the reference JSON itself
is garbled, fix it first against the source PDF with vision-mode verification.

### Scale
- Total garbled entries: ~400 (grep -c "split_from_garbled" index.html)
- Affected cults: most non-common cults (Storm, Lunar, Praxian, Darkness pantheons)
- Already fixed: 7 Mothers - Jakaleel (commit 4cd024e)
close_reason: Closed

title: feat: Agent-browser-first API refactor — expose App.agent.getState(), granular actions, assertion helpers
notes:
## Agent-Browser-First Refactor Plan

### Goal
Make index.html natively automatable by agent-browser without DOM scraping or fragile eval expressions.

### Phase 1: State Query API
Add read-only API methods that return structured JSON:
- App.agent.getState() -> Full CharacterData + computed attributes
- App.agent.getUIState() -> {currentStep, visiblePickers, errors, toasts}
- App.agent.getValidation() -> {valid: bool, errors: [...], warnings: [...]}
- App.agent.getMagicState() -> {cultType, devotionalPool, boundSpiritSlots, sorceryResource, selectedMiracles, selectedSpells, selectedSpirits}

### Phase 2: Granular Action API
Add mutation methods that return success/error:
- App.agent.setStep(n) -> navigate to step
- App.agent.selectCult(name) -> select cult, trigger picker rendering
- App.agent.toggleSpell(name) -> toggle sorcery spell
- App.agent.toggleSpirit(name) -> toggle bound spirit
- App.agent.selectMiracle(name) -> select/deselect miracle
- App.agent.allocateSkill(name, points) -> allocate points to a skill
- App.agent.setCharacteristic(name, value) -> set a single characteristic

### Phase 3: Assertion Helpers
Add self-checking methods for automated testing:
- App.agent.assertSpellCount() -> {selected, limit, spells}
- App.agent.assertSpiritSlots() -> {used, limit, spirits}
- App.agent.assertMiracles() -> {selected, available, miracles}
- App.agent.assertPlayMode() -> {sections, errors, missing}
- App.agent.assertPDFExport() -> {success, errors}

### Phase 4: Event Log
- App.agent.getEventLog() -> [{action, timestamp, result, error}, ...]
- Auto-records all agent API calls with results

### Success Criteria
- All 30 existing E2E assertions still pass
- New API methods documented in .rpiv/guidance/agent-api.md
- agent-browser eval expressions become one-liners instead of multi-line IIFEs
- No DOM parsing needed for any test assertion

### Estimated Scope
- ~200 lines of new JS in index.html
- Update test-agent-api.mjs to use new API
- Update .rpiv/guidance/agent-api.md
close_reason: Closed
